content.text:
I'll start by understanding the current state of the project by reading the harness files.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Read">
<parameter name="file_path">/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/claude-progress.txt</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<results>
<output name="Read">
# Claude Progress Log

## Project: MMS (Metro Integrated Planning System)
## Purpose: Track session history for harness continuity

---

## Session Log

### Session 001 (2025-12-12) ~2 hours - PRODUCTIVE
**Focus:** Project setup and validation
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ F001: File structure exists
- ✓ F002: Vite dev server runs
- ✓ F003: BlueprintJS configured

**Decisions:**
- Pure HTML POC first (no build tools)
- React 18 required for BlueprintJS 6 compatibility

**Blockers Resolved:**
- Monorepo config issues → Created pure HTML POC

**State:** Clean, F001-F003 complete

---

### Session 002 (2025-12-13) ~4 hours - MAJOR PROGRESS
**Focus:** Core rendering implementation
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ F101: Vite + React 18 foundation (re-verified)
- ✓ F102: BlueprintJS 6 integration with Navbar
- ✓ F103: R3F 8.x integration with Canvas
- ✓ F104: Mock data generator (50 trains × 10 years)
- ✓ F105: MesoLayer with InstancedMesh (60k instances)
- ✓ F106: Pan/zoom with OrbitControls + orthographic camera
- ✓ F107: LOD state machine in useViewport hook
- ✓ F108: MacroLayer heatmap (InstancedMesh, simplified)
- ✓ F109: MicroLayer task cards with drei Html
- ✓ F110: Smooth LOD transitions (opacity-based)

**Technical Decisions:**
- MacroLayer: InstancedMesh approach (simpler than shader)
- MicroLayer: drei Html with viewport culling (efficient)
- LOD Transitions: Opacity crossfade over 300ms

**Key Files Created/Modified:**
- apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts - Full type definitions
- apps/canvas/src/data/generator.ts - Mock data with render transform
- apps/canvas/src/hooks/useViewport.ts - LOD state machine
- apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MacroLayer.tsx - Heatmap cells
- apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx - InstancedMesh
- apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx - HTML cards
- apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx - Layer orchestration
- apps/canvas/src/App.tsx - BlueprintJS layout

**Blockers Resolved:**
- Type errors in generator → Fixed ISO date handling
- InstancedMesh colors → Used THREE.Color properly
- drei Html positioning → Fixed coordinate transform

**State:** Clean, all foundation and core rendering complete

---

### Session 003 (2025-12-13) ~2 hours - UI INTEGRATION
**Focus:** UI components and polish
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ F111: Train sidebar with vertical scroll sync
- ✓ F112: Timeline header (DateHeader) with adaptive granularity
- ✓ F113: BlueprintJS zoom controls (ButtonGroup)
- ✓ F114: Visual conflict indicators (red styling)

**Technical Decisions:**
- Sidebar: Smooth virtualization with synced camera
- Timeline: Years at macro, quarters at meso, months at micro
- Zoom controls: +/- buttons with 1.5x step

**Key Files Created:**
- apps/canvas/src/components/TrainSidebar.tsx
- apps/canvas/src/components/DateHeader.tsx
- apps/canvas/src/components/ZoomControls.tsx
- apps/canvas/src/components/LODIndicator.tsx

**State:** Clean, F101-F114 complete

---

### Session 004 (2025-12-13) ~1.5 hours - POLISH
**Focus:** Final polish and visual parity
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ F115: Auto-dismissing onboarding toast
- ✓ F116: Performance optimization (stable at 60fps)
- ✓ F117: Visual parity with POC reference

**Technical Decisions:**
- Instructions: BlueprintJS Toaster, auto-dismiss 5s
- Performance: Already optimized via InstancedMesh
- Visual parity: Verified all 3 LOD levels match POC

**Final State:** ALL FEATURES COMPLETE (F101-F117)

---

### Session 005 (2025-12-14) ~2 hours - CRITICAL FIX
**Focus:** Camera and visual verification
**Issue Found:** Camera was looking at wrong location - content not visible
**Root Cause:** Camera positioned at (0,0,5) looking at origin, but content starts at positive X/Y

**Features Re-verified after fix:**
- ✓ F103: R3F canvas now shows content
- ✓ F105-F110: All layers visible and working
- ✓ All visual tests passing

**Technical Fix:**
- Set initial camera position to center of data (X: ~1825, Y: ~600)
- Added useEffect to position camera on mount
- Content now visible immediately on load

**Key Insight:** Visual testing caught what code review missed.

**State:** Clean, all features verified visually

---

### Session 006 (2025-12-14) ~45min - ZOOM CENTERING FIX
**Focus:** Zoom behavior fix
**Issue Found:** Zoom was centering on screen center, not cursor position
**User Expectation:** Zoom should center on mouse cursor (like Google Maps)

**Fix Applied:**
- Modified handleWheel in InfiniteCanvas.tsx
- Now calculates cursor position in world coordinates
- Adjusts camera position to keep cursor point stable during zoom

**Technical Details:**
- Convert cursor screen position to world coordinates before zoom
- Apply zoom factor
- Convert same screen position to new world coordinates
- Adjust camera by the difference

**State:** Clean, zoom behavior matches user expectation

---

### Session 007 (2025-12-14) ~2 hours - PAN BOUNDARIES
**Focus:** Pan constraint implementation
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ F200: Horizontal pan boundaries (0 to totalDays)
- ✓ F201: Vertical pan boundaries (0 to totalTrains)

**Technical Implementation:**
- Added clampPosition function to InfiniteCanvas
- Calculates visible viewport size based on zoom
- Constrains camera so content edges align with viewport edges
- Applied after every pan and zoom operation

**Key Formula:**
```
minX = visibleWidth / 2
maxX = totalWidth - visibleWidth / 2
minY = visibleHeight / 2
maxY = totalHeight - visibleHeight / 2
```

**State:** Clean, pan boundaries working correctly

---

### Session 008 (2025-12-14) ~1 hour - ZOOM BOUNDARY FIX
**Focus:** Fix zoom boundaries that were too restrictive
**Issue:** Zoom was clamped 0.1-10 but camera made it effectively 0.2-1.0
**Root Cause:** Zoom calculations used visible size which created circular dependency

**Fix Applied:**
- Separated zoom limits (0.1-10) from pan constraints
- Pan constraints now correctly use camera.zoom, not internal state
- Content stays in view at all zoom levels

**State:** Clean, zoom range 0.1-10 fully usable

---

### Session 009 (2025-12-14) ~30min - PAN SYNC FIX
**Focus:** Fix sidebar/header sync after panning
**Issue:** TrainSidebar and DateHeader not updating during pan
**Root Cause:** Components were using stale camera position

**Fix Applied:**
- Added useFrame hook in InfiniteCanvas to broadcast camera position
- TrainSidebar and DateHeader now receive live camera updates
- All UI elements stay synchronized

**State:** Clean, pan sync working

---

### Session 010 (2025-12-14) ~1 hour - BOUNDARY REFINEMENT
**Focus:** Improve pan boundary behavior
**Issue:** Jerky snapping when hitting boundaries
**Solution:** Smooth elastic boundaries with spring-back

**Technical Details:**
- Allow slight overshoot (10% of visible area)
- Spring animation back to valid range
- Feels more natural and responsive

**State:** Clean, boundaries feel polished

---

### Session 011 (2025-12-14) ~2 hours - SIDEBAR SCROLL SYNC
**Focus:** Bidirectional sidebar scroll synchronization
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ Scrolling sidebar pans canvas
- ✓ Panning canvas scrolls sidebar
- ✓ No infinite loops between the two

**Technical Implementation:**
- Used ref to track scroll source (user vs programmatic)
- Debounced scroll events to prevent thrashing
- Synced via shared viewport state

**State:** Clean, bidirectional sync working

---

### Session 012 (2025-12-15) ~3 hours - HARNESS ARCHITECTURE OVERHAUL
**Focus:** Multi-package harness design research and documentation

**Research Completed:**
- Read Anthropic's official harness documentation
- Analyzed parallel execution patterns for monorepos
- Designed package-level harness architecture

**Decisions Made:**
- Each package gets its own progress.txt and features.json
- Root orchestrator spawns sub-agents per package
- AGPH (Auto-Generated Progress from Harness) pattern adopted

**Key Artifacts Created:**
- packages/harness-orchestrator/README.md - Full orchestrator design
- packages/harness-orchestrator/ARCHITECTURE.md - Technical details
- packages/harness-orchestrator/features.json - Orchestrator backlog
- packages/harness-orchestrator/progress.txt - Orchestrator progress
- packages/harness-dashboard/features.json - Dashboard backlog
- packages/harness-dashboard/progress.txt - Dashboard progress

**New Pattern: AGPH**
Auto-Generated Progress from Harness:
- Each package has a features.json (machine-readable)
- progress.txt is auto-generated summary
- Orchestrator aggregates all package states
- Dashboard renders real-time progress

**State:** Documentation complete, ready for implementation

---

### Session 013 (2025-12-15) ~2 hours - UX RESEARCH
**Focus:** Pan/zoom UX patterns research
**Artifact Created:** docs/UX_RESEARCH.md

**Research Findings:**
1. Pan-only by default (hold Spacebar for temporary hand tool)
2. Wheel zooms by default, Shift+Wheel for vertical scroll
3. Zoom toward cursor (already implemented)
4. Inertia/momentum for natural feel
5. Rubber-banding at boundaries

**Platforms Analyzed:**
- Figma, Miro, FigJam (design tools)
- Google Maps, Apple Maps (mapping)
- iOS/macOS trackpad gestures

**State:** Research documented, ready for implementation

---

### Session 014 (2025-12-15) ~30min - HARNESS DASHBOARD INIT
**Focus:** Initialize harness-dashboard package structure
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ HD001: Package initialization with proper structure
- ✓ HD002: AGPH integration (features.json + progress.txt)

**Files Created:**
- packages/harness-dashboard/package.json
- packages/harness-dashboard/tsconfig.json
- packages/harness-dashboard/README.md
- packages/harness-dashboard/src/index.ts (placeholder)

**State:** Clean, package scaffolded

---

### Session 015 (2025-12-16) ~2 hours - QA TESTING PROTOCOL
**Focus:** Comprehensive QA protocol for WebGL/Canvas testing
**Artifact Created:** docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md

**Protocol Covers:**
1. Visual regression testing for all LOD levels
2. Interaction testing (pan, zoom, click, keyboard)
3. Performance benchmarks (60fps target)
4. Cross-browser compatibility matrix
5. Accessibility audit checklist

**Key Decisions:**
- Playwright MCP as primary automation tool
- Screenshot comparison for visual regression
- Console error monitoring mandatory
- Performance budget: 16ms frame time

**State:** Protocol documented, ready for implementation

---

### Session 016 (2025-12-16) ~1.5 hours - MOMENTUM PANNING
**Focus:** Implement inertia/momentum for panning (F118)
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ F118: Momentum panning with velocity decay

**Technical Implementation:**
- Track velocity during drag (last N positions)
- On drag end, continue motion with exponential decay
- Decay factor: 0.95 per frame
- Stop threshold: velocity < 0.1

**Key Files Modified:**
- apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx

**Formula:**
```typescript
velocity *= DECAY_FACTOR; // 0.95
position += velocity * deltaTime;
if (Math.abs(velocity) < STOP_THRESHOLD) velocity = 0;
```

**State:** Clean, momentum panning working smoothly

---

## Current Status

**Completed Features:** F101-F118, F200-F201
**Next Up:** F119 (rubber-banding boundaries) or orchestrator work

**Production App:** apps/canvas (fully functional)
- All 3 LOD levels working
- Pan/zoom with momentum
- Sidebar and header sync
- Visual parity with POC

**Known Issues:** None

**Tech Debt:** None critical

---

## Quick Reference

### Commands
```bash
# Run production app
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev

# View POC reference
open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html

# Type check
bun run typecheck
```

### Key Files
- apps/canvas/src/App.tsx - Main layout
- apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx - Core rendering
- apps/canvas/src/hooks/useViewport.ts - State machine
- apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/*.tsx - LOD layers

---

## Session Template (Copy for new sessions)

```
### Session XXX (YYYY-MM-DD) ~Xh - TITLE
**Focus:** What you're working on
**Features Completed:**
- ✓ FXXX: Description

**Technical Decisions:**
- Decision: Rationale

**Key Files Created/Modified:**
- path/to/file.ts - Description

**Blockers Resolved:**
- Issue → Solution

**State:** Clean/Dirty, notes
```
</output>
</invoke>
</results>

I've read the harness files and understand the current state of the project. The MMS (Metro Integrated Planning System) is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet maintenance planning with a "Semantic Zoom" UX (Google Earth-style).

**Current Status Summary:**
- **Completed Features:** F101-F118, F200-F201 (18 features complete)
- **Next Up:** F119 (rubber-banding boundaries), F120 (keyboard pan/zoom)
- **Production App:** `apps/canvas` is fully functional with all 3 LOD levels, pan/zoom with momentum, sidebar/header sync

**Tech Stack:**
- Vite 5.x + React 18.3.1 + TypeScript
- BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (UI components)
- React Three Fiber 8.x + drei 9.x + Three.js 0.170.x (WebGL)

**Key Commands:**
```bash
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev  # Run dev server at localhost:5173
```

I'm ready to assist. What would you like me to explore or design?
